title: Update Info page Hebrew studio content with RTL rendering

requirements.summary: Replace the Info page studio description with the provided expanded Hebrew content while preserving the existing layout and Tailwind styling patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /info shows the new Hebrew heading and three expanded paragraphs (replacing the previous shorter text).
The overall layout remains consistent with the current Info page structure (Header, main container spacing, max width wrapper, section spacing, and typography classes remain in the same style).
No English studio-description section/content is introduced on the Info page.

file_operations.description: Update the Hebrew studio section copy to match the user-provided expanded content, keeping the current page scaffold (Header/Footer, main container paddings, max-width wrapper) and the existing typography/spacing Tailwind class patterns. Ensure the content includes the new heading plus three paragraphs, and remove the prior shorter studio text without adding any English studio-description content.

requirements.summary: Ensure the updated Hebrew content on the Info page renders correctly in RTL with proper alignment and bold emphasis using valid JSX.
acceptanceCriteria: The Hebrew content on /info is rendered RTL (e.g., the content container has RTL direction and right alignment).
Bold emphasis in the provided content is preserved and rendered correctly (using the project’s existing JSX/HTML conventions for emphasis).

There are no malformed/invalid tags introduced from the pasted HTML (e.g., no broken

tags or stray line breaks that break rendering).

file_operations.description:
Preserve/enforce RTL rendering on the Hebrew section (e.g., keep dir="rtl" and right-aligned layout conventions) and ensure emphasis from the provided HTML () is represented using valid JSX emphasis (e.g., ) consistent with the existing codebase. Do not paste raw HTML; translate it into well-formed JSX (

as already used by the page, plus

blocks) to avoid malformed tags.
